title: Mindful Eating Reflection Log
description: Track your mindful and intuitive eating practice alongside mood and emotional awareness. Research shows that mindful eating is associated with lower disordered eating, lower depression, and better body image.
instructions: Complete this log after at least one mindful meal per day. Focus on awareness and self-compassion rather than judgment. This is about building a healthier relationship with food, not restricting.

fields.label: Which meal did you eat mindfully today?
How hungry were you before eating?
What was your emotional state before eating?
Were you eating in response to emotions rather than physical hunger?
How present were you during the meal? (not distracted by screens, work, etc.)
How satisfied did you feel after eating?
Were you kind to yourself about your food choices today?
What did you notice about your eating experience today?
fields.placeholder: e.g., I noticed I eat much faster when stressed. When I slowed down, I enjoyed the meal more and felt satisfied sooner.

disclaimer: This log is designed to build awareness, not restriction. If tracking your eating triggers guilt, anxiety, or disordered eating patterns, please stop using this tool and consult a healthcare provider. This is not appropriate for individuals with active eating disorders. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
